title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust · Recovery Case: Gita Untrusted Repository Defense
summary: A cloned repository proposes hooks, includeIf config, credential helpers, aliases, filters, safe.directory exceptions, and a maintenance schedule on a shared build host. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita untrusted repository defense increment in repository trust and local automation threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
Choose credential helpers, URL matching, useHttpPath, askpass, environment, token scope, and erasure behavior without writing secrets into repository state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Untrusted Repository Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G96 is a theory in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Untrusted Repository Defense contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Untrusted Repository Defense while producing repository trust and local automation threat model.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-untrusted-repository-defense — Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository
git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\."
# expected: scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value
# observation: a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data
# changed-case: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential
# transfer-limit: inspect unknown repositories in isolation with hooks, filters, aliases, and external helpers disabled

steps.title: Build Gita Untrusted Repository Defense evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-17zoy7" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Untrusted Repository Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-17zoy7
# scenario-key: trace-17zoy7 probe-7yoz71 boundary-zoy717 owner-17zoy7x fault-17zoy7z
# before-ref: main=a11ce17 candidate=b00b1y7
# hypothesis: conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository
git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\."
# expected: scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value
# observation: a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data
# changed-case: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception
# after-ref: main=a11ce17 candidate=c0ffee7
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential
# transfer-limit: inspect unknown repositories in isolation with hooks, filters, aliases, and external helpers disabled
